title: Mid-cycle seat add led to prorated surcharge removing negotiated volume discount on consolidated invoice
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer SiftMail reports a charge on their March consolidated invoice that appears to negate their negotiated volume discount after adding 12 seats mid-cycle. The invoice shows a prorated "surcharge" line where per-seat pricing reverted to list price for the prorated portion rather than applying the ...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer SiftMail reports a charge on their March consolidated invoice that appears to negate their negotiated volume discount after adding 12 seats mid-cycle. The invoice shows a prorated "surcharge" line where per-seat pricing reverted to list price for the prorated portion rather than applying the customer's tiered volume discount.

Impact:
- Customer sees a $4,322 unexpected line item affecting month close and internal chargebacks.
- Customer concerned this will recur on future mid-cycle seat changes.

Environment:
- Enterprise org on reserved/dedicated plan with negotiated tier: 31+ seats => $1.80/seat; 11-30 seats => $2.10/seat.
- New seats added 2026-03-05T15:40:12Z (12 seats) via admin console. Billing cycle starts 1st of month.

Requested outcome:
- Clarify why prorate used list price for added seats instead of applying the negotiated tier once total seat count...

title: Configuring sustained vs burst quota for multiplexed ingestion workers to avoid 429s
preview:
Issue summary: Customer Arcadia Labs reports recurring 429 throttles during steady-state ingestion runs from a fleet of short-lived uploader workers. They see 429s even when token usage looks within expected monthly/rolling quotas.

Impact: Intermittent failed uploads (10-25% of requests during 1–2 hour ingestion wi...
text:
Issue summary: Customer Arcadia Labs reports recurring 429 throttles during steady-state ingestion runs from a fleet of short-lived uploader workers. They see 429s even when token usage looks within expected monthly/rolling quotas.

Impact: Intermittent failed uploads (10-25% of requests during 1–2 hour ingestion windows), delays in downstream indexing, and elevated retry traffic causing secondary waves of throttles. No billing disputes opened, but customer is concerned about reliability for nightly syncs.

Environment: self_serve tenant in prod (us-east primary, eu-west failover). Workers spawn ephemeral API keys (lifetime ~5–10 minutes) and open 20–50 concurrent connections per worker process. Ingestion pattern: steady sustained throughput ~120 requests/s for 90–120 minutes, with microbursts when workers restart.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Create N ephemeral keys and fan out 300 worker...

title: Ephemeral connection spike triggering GPU attach backoff and mid-window throughput sag in dedicated pool
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer NimbleAI reports a mid-day p50/p99 throughput drop in their dedicated pool. Investigations show a burst of short-lived connections from a small tenant group coincided with repeated GPU attach failures and exponential attach backoff from the allocator, leading to effective slot starvation and ...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer NimbleAI reports a mid-day p50/p99 throughput drop in their dedicated pool. Investigations show a burst of short-lived connections from a small tenant group coincided with repeated GPU attach failures and exponential attach backoff from the allocator, leading to effective slot starvation and reduced batched throughput.

Impact:
- Customer-facing endpoints experienced ~30% drop in throughput and p99 increased from 280ms to 1.2s for 20 minutes during the spike window.
- Requests were not returning 5xx but observed long queue times and autoscaler reacted with rapid scale-down/scale-up cycles (oscillation).

Environment:
- Dedicated pool for NimbleAI (reserved GPUs), prod - us-east-1
- Serving runtime v3.8.1, autoscaler v2.4.0, sidecar v1.9.

Timeline (local):
- 2026-03-12 11:46 UTC: Gradual p99 drift begins.
- 2026-03-12 11:52 UTC: Connection spike (short-lived st...

title: Traffic diverted to standby variant causing long-tail latency and intermittent 504s for enterprise billing service
preview: Issue summary: Starting ~2026-03-13 10:20 UTC NimbusFinance reported elevated end-to-end latency and intermittent 504s on their nightly billing job. Impact: 30% of billing requests experienced >5s latency and ~6% returned 504s over a 2 hour window, causing downstream reconciliation failures and customer alerts. Envi...
text: Issue summary: Starting ~2026-03-13 10:20 UTC NimbusFinance reported elevated end-to-end latency and intermittent 504s on their nightly billing job. Impact: 30% of billing requests experienced >5s latency and ~6% returned 504s over a 2 hour window, causing downstream reconciliation failures and customer alerts. Environment: Dedicated tenant (NimbusFinance) on prod-us-east, routing policy: cost-aware primary->standby diversion enabled for load smoothing. Observed behavior: Traffic was routed to "standby" variant replicas that reported as READY but were still performing model KV population and kernel JIT; this produced serial warmups and long-tail p99. The issue appears tied to a routing decision that prefers standby racks when observed primary CPU pressure exceeded a small threshold.

title: Device-trust approval delay triggers repeated console access-request loop
preview:
Issue summary:
A subset of internal users attempting to sign in to the Redwood Console are stuck in an access-request loop: the console shows "Waiting for device approval" and the platform repeatedly creates new pending access-request records instead of updating the existing request. Approvers are not receiving stab...
text:
Issue summary:
A subset of internal users attempting to sign in to the Redwood Console are stuck in an access-request loop: the console shows "Waiting for device approval" and the platform repeatedly creates new pending access-request records instead of updating the existing request. Approvers are not receiving stable notifications, and on-call pages are being generated for duplicate requests.

Impact:
- ~12 engineers across two squads reported inability to complete console login via SSO (device approval required).
- Flood of duplicate access-request tickets in the admin queue causing approver overload and delayed approvals.
- Temporary manual approvals required for critical oncall responders. No production compute or model serving impact observed.

Environment:
- Prod sign-in flow through Okta + Device Trust + internal SSO bridge (sso-bridge). Region: us-west. Affected users appear t...

title: Duplicate final choice and lingering partial tokens after mobile app background/reconnect (WebSocket)
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer ClipNote reports that when their mobile app (iOS) backgrounds and then reconnects over WebSocket, the final choice from the model is sometimes duplicated and a short sequence of partial tokens reappears after the UI believes the response is finished. This occurs sporadically and appears corre...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer ClipNote reports that when their mobile app (iOS) backgrounds and then reconnects over WebSocket, the final choice from the model is sometimes duplicated and a short sequence of partial tokens reappears after the UI believes the response is finished. This occurs sporadically and appears correlated with mobile network transitions (Wi-Fi -> cellular) and device sleep/resume.

Impact:
- Affects production users of ClipNote mobile app (estimated ~0.6% of sessions during last 48h).
- Duplicated final output leads to confusing UI (repeated sentence) and occasionally triggers client-side logic that posts the same message twice.
- Customer classifies as P1 / Sev1 due to visible UX regressions on their paid tier.

Environment:
- Customer tier: enterprise (dedicated capacity).
- Region: us-east (cluster rwi-prod-1).
- Mobile: iOS 15/16, app uses Redwood mobile SDK v0.9.3...

title: Provision 'Model Safety Review' pod: Confluence spaces, delegated admin groups, and timezone-aware ephemeral Slack standup channels
preview:
Issue summary:
We need a standardized project bootstrap for the new cross-functional 'Model Safety Review' pods. The request covers creating scoped Confluence spaces with templated pages, provisioning delegated Confluence admin groups per pod, and creating a set of ephemeral Slack channels for async/standup coordina...
text:
Issue summary:
We need a standardized project bootstrap for the new cross-functional 'Model Safety Review' pods. The request covers creating scoped Confluence spaces with templated pages, provisioning delegated Confluence admin groups per pod, and creating a set of ephemeral Slack channels for async/standup coordination with timezone-aware naming and archival automation.

Background and rationale:
- Several new pods will start working on safety evaluations and need consistent docs and low-friction collaboration spaces.
- Current ad-hoc creation leads to inconsistent permissions, missing watchers, and manual cleanup.
- Pods operate across APAC/EMEA/NA timezones; standup channels should be grouped by timezone to reduce noise.

Requested deliverables (high level):
1) Create a Confluence parent space named "Model Safety Review - <pod-name>" for each new pod (template to be applied). The s...

title: RRB-17: Private installer rollback loop / stuck rollback during upgrade
preview:
Issue summary:
Customers upgrading Redwood Private 2.8.0 -> 2.9.0 can enter an infinite rollback retry loop. UI shows "Rollback in progress" and never completes.

Impact:
- Upgrade/rollback does not converge; customer cluster remains in degraded state.
- Support load + customer trust risk.

Environment:
- Private in...
text:
Issue summary:
Customers upgrading Redwood Private 2.8.0 -> 2.9.0 can enter an infinite rollback retry loop. UI shows "Rollback in progress" and never completes.

Impact:
- Upgrade/rollback does not converge; customer cluster remains in degraded state.
- Support load + customer trust risk.

Environment:
- Private installer 2.9.0
- Kubernetes (various), observed in us-east + eu-west

Suspected root cause:
Rollback cleanup hook exits before clearing rollback lock (ConfigMap `installer-rollback-lock`), causing controller to retry indefinitely.

Workaround (customer-safe):
1) Confirm namespace `redwood-private`
2) `kubectl -n redwood-private delete cm installer-rollback-lock`
3) `kubectl -n redwood-private rollout restart deploy/installer-controller`

Acceptance criteria / fix:
- Always best-effort clear lock
- Add TTL + loop detection to stop retries and surface actionable status
- Add m...

title: Scheduler queue buildup delays streaming start times (Hosted us-west)
preview:
Issue summary:
First token times for streaming are extremely high; queue depth climbing. Some clients retry aggressively, compounding load.

Impact:
Customer-visible latency; timeouts at gateway.

Environment:
prod us-west; hosted tier.

Notes:
Suspected regression in scheduler admission control under high concurrency.

title: Orion Defense requesting offline installer bundle for closed network Private deployment
preview:
Issue summary
Orion Defense cannot allow any outbound internet access from their Kubernetes cluster (closed enclave). They are requesting an offline installer bundle (images + charts) and a checklist documenting any outbound assumptions.

Impact
Blocks Private proof-of-concept until packaging is available for discon...
text:
Issue summary
Orion Defense cannot allow any outbound internet access from their Kubernetes cluster (closed enclave). They are requesting an offline installer bundle (images + charts) and a checklist documenting any outbound assumptions.

Impact
Blocks Private proof-of-concept until packaging is available for disconnected install.

Environment
On-prem Kubernetes, internal-only DNS/NTP, internal PKI. Public OCSP/CRL not permitted.

Customer request
- Provide container images in a format they can import into internal registry.
- Provide pinned digests + checksums.
- Provide upgrade cadence expectations (quarterly windows).

Comments
2025-02-14 Jordan Blake (reporter): Customer says "no outbound" and "no proxy". Need to make sure nothing phones home.
2025-02-15 Hiro Tanaka (assignee): We can generate a v0 bundle from the current private-installer release. Will include digest list + check...

title: Go SDK reports 'invalid API key' when key loaded from file includes trailing newline
preview: Issue summary: Customer running Redwood Private deployment reports Go SDK errors 'invalid API key' after moving from env var injection to reading key from a mounted file. They confirmed the key works via curl when manually copied. Impact: app cannot authenticate to private control plane. Steps: (1) read file content...
text: Issue summary: Customer running Redwood Private deployment reports Go SDK errors 'invalid API key' after moving from env var injection to reading key from a mounted file. They confirmed the key works via curl when manually copied. Impact: app cannot authenticate to private control plane. Steps: (1) read file contents (2) pass string to redwood.NewClient(apiKey) (3) API returns 401 with message invalid_api_key. Hypothesis: key string contains trailing newline or spaces.
